metadata.purpose: Single source of truth for the study-area AOI. Every script (clip_climate_normals.py, gvmi_baseline.py, future LFMC inference) reads its extent from this file so the corridor is defined in exactly one place.
metadata.crs: EPSG:4326 (WGS84 lon/lat decimal degrees), per the GeoJSON spec.
metadata.block_split: Three mauka-to-makai blocks split by longitude (elevation runs roughly east=upcountry/high to west=coast/low), separated by 0.010 deg (~1 km) buffer gaps so no grid cell is shared or adjacent across folds. Use these as leakage-safe spatial cross-validation folds. See aoi/corridor.md for the full rationale.
metadata.author: Samuel Dameg (SammyCode002)

properties.name: Kula-to-Kihei corridor
properties.description: Mauka-to-makai corridor from upper Kula (Haleakala slopes) down to Waiohuli Kai / South Kihei coast. This is the polygon clip scripts read.
Coastal Kihei block. Western third of the corridor. CV fold 1.
Mid-slope block. Middle third of the corridor, separated from its neighbors by 0.010 deg buffer gaps. CV fold 2.
Upper Kula block. Eastern third of the corridor. CV fold 3.
